版本前瞻 Version Preview

1.1 梦行回廊版本前瞻：6月3日前资源囤积、队伍准备与内容优先级 1.1 Dreamwalk Corridor Preview: Resource Stockpiling, Team Prep and Priorities Before June 3

发布时间：Published: 2026-05-24 作者：异环攻略站编辑部Author: NTE Guide Editorial Team 阅读时间：10 分钟Read time: 10 min

攻略速览

1.1「梦行回廊 / Dreamwalk Corridor」已在 2026 年 5 月下旬公开，计划于 2026 年 6 月 3 日上线。由于该内容在当前时间点仍属于前瞻阶段，本文不把未上线玩法写成实测结论，而是从 1.0 已确定的养成结构、开服资源节奏和版本更新前常见准备项出发，给出 5 月底到 6 月初最稳妥的资源安排。

前置条件与接取方式

适用时间

2026-05-24 至 1.1 版本上线前

核心目标

保留抽卡资源，补齐主队等级、技能与基础装备

风险提醒

未上线内容以官方最终公告和游戏内实装为准

版本前准备顺序

先保底线，再追上限

1

清空限时奖励与邮件

优先确认环石、抽卡券、体力道具和活动兑换币

开服补偿、登录奖励和限时活动通常有领取期限。准备 1.1 前先检查邮箱、活动页和兑换商店，避免把可转化为抽卡或养成的资源遗留到过期。

2

保留至少一轮角色池预算

除非当前池能直接补强主队，否则不要把资源压到低优先级命座

版本更新前最容易出现冲动补抽。对普通玩家来说，保留足够抽数应对新角色、新弧盘或强功能位，比继续追小幅提升更稳。

3

把一队练到可稳定清日常

主C、功能副C、生存位和环合位优先成型

新版本通常会追加活动关卡或挑战目标。与其平均拉多个半成品角色，不如先确保一队能稳定处理材料本、异象委托和活动前置。

4

提前清理地图传送网络

维特海默塔、电话亭和世界BOSS传送点优先点亮

如果 1.1 任务需要跨区跑图，完整传送网络会节省大量时间。5 月底最值得补的是传送点、拍照支线和影响探索度的关键收集。

5

体力集中刷通用材料

优先刷角色突破、技能书、武器突破和通用货币

新角色专属材料在上线前未必能提前准备，通用材料却几乎一定会消耗。体力不足时不要赌冷门本，优先补所有队伍都能用到的底层资源。

版本前常见失误

资源焦虑与信息误读

前瞻期最大问题不是打不过关卡，而是把未经确认的爆料当作实装数据。所有新角色数值、活动倍率和奖励池都应等游戏内确认后再做最终规划。

  • 不要为了传闻中的环境变化提前拆掉现有队伍，1.0 主力仍是你进入 1.1 的基础。
  • 不要把所有体力刷成单一属性材料，版本变动前保留通用资源更稳。
  • 关注官方公告中的上线日期、维护补偿、活动持续时间和兑换商店关闭时间。

完成奖励

环石规划 抽卡券保留 通用突破材料 探索进度 活动入场效率

1.1 前最合理的策略是：抽卡资源留给新信息，体力投入通用成长，地图进度补到不拖任务。这样无论梦行回廊最终偏战斗、探索还是剧情活动，都能减少临时补课成本。

完整解析

截至 2026 年 5 月 24 日，1.1「梦行回廊」的关键信息是版本已经公开且计划在 2026 年 6 月 3 日上线。这个时间点适合做准备，但不适合把尚未实装的角色倍率、敌人机制或活动收益写成确定结论。真正影响账号体验的，仍然是 1.0 阶段已经确定的基础建设：一队强度、传送网络、资源库存和抽卡预算。

对零氪和月卡玩家来说，5 月底最重要的是停止低收益消耗。已经拥有能稳定清日常与主线的主C时，不建议为了小幅伤害提升继续追高觉醒。新版本前的抽卡价值来自选择权，手里保留一轮预算，才能在新角色、复刻、专武或强工具人出现时做决策。

体力分配建议偏保守。角色突破、技能材料、武器突破、方斯和经验素材永远不会浪费；而某些看似会契合新角色的属性材料，在未确认前很容易刷过量。背包里通用资源越充足，新版本上线后的试错空间越大。

探索进度也值得提前补。很多玩家会低估电话亭、维特海默塔和世界BOSS传送点的价值。版本任务如果需要跨区移动，传送点缺失会让流程被跑图时间拖慢。5 月底清理这些基础点位，比盲刷随机副词条更能提升上线后的效率。

Guide Snapshot

Version 1.1, Dreamwalk Corridor, was publicly previewed in late May 2026 and is planned for June 3, 2026. Because the content is still unreleased, this guide does not treat leaked or unimplemented details as confirmed. It uses the known 1.0 growth structure, launch resource flow and normal pre-patch preparation logic to recommend the safest plan from late May to early June.

Requirements and Entry

Timeframe

May 24, 2026 until the 1.1 release

Main goal

Keep pull currency, finish your main team and stock universal materials

Risk note

Unreleased content should always defer to final official notices and in-game implementation

Pre-Version Preparation Order

Secure the floor before chasing the ceiling

1

Claim limited rewards and mail

Check Annulith, pull tickets, stamina items and event currency first.

Compensation, login rewards and event shops often expire. Before 1.1, review mail, event pages and exchange shops so convertible resources do not disappear unused.

2

Keep at least one character-banner budget

Do not sink resources into low-priority awakenings unless the current banner directly fixes your main team.

For most players, keeping enough pulls for new characters, new Arc Plates or strong utility roles is safer than chasing a small damage increase before patch information is complete.

3

Build one team that clears dailies

Main DPS, functional sub-DPS, survival slot and reaction/enabler slot should be usable.

New versions often add event stages or challenge objectives. A stable first team is more valuable than several half-built characters.

4

Clean the teleport network early

Activate Wertheimer Towers, phone booths and world-boss teleports.

If 1.1 quests require cross-district travel, a complete teleport network saves significant time. Late May is a good point to finish teleports, photo side quests and key exploration points.

5

Spend stamina on universal materials

Farm ascension, skill books, weapon upgrades and currency.

New-character exclusive materials may not be pre-farmable, but universal materials are almost always consumed. Avoid gambling stamina on narrow materials before official data is final.

Common Pre-Version Mistake

Resource anxiety and misread information

The biggest preview-period problem is planning around unconfirmed data. New character numbers, event multipliers and reward pools should be finalized only after they are visible in game.

  • Do not dismantle your current 1.0 team because of rumors.
  • Do not convert all stamina into one attribute material before new requirements are confirmed.
  • Track official launch dates, maintenance compensation, event duration and shop closing time.

Rewards

Annulith plan Saved pull tickets Universal ascension materials Exploration progress Faster event entry

The best pre-1.1 plan is simple: keep pull currency flexible, invest stamina into universal growth, and finish enough map progress that new quests do not stall. Whether Dreamwalk Corridor leans combat, exploration or story, this reduces last-minute catch-up costs.

Full Analysis

As of May 24, 2026, the key confirmed point is that 1.1 Dreamwalk Corridor has been announced and is planned for June 3, 2026. This is a preparation window, not a reason to write unreleased character values or enemy mechanics as facts.

For free-to-play and monthly-card players, late May is about stopping low-return spending. If your main DPS already clears dailies and story content, chasing small awakenings is usually less valuable than keeping one full budget for new information.

Stamina planning should stay conservative. Character ascension, skill materials, weapon upgrades, Fons and experience items are unlikely to be wasted, while unconfirmed attribute materials can easily be over-farmed.

Exploration progress is also worth finishing. Missing phone booths, Wertheimer Towers and world-boss teleports can slow patch quests more than weak sub-stats. Clean the infrastructure first, then chase random stat upgrades.
